step: Inspected task 001/006/007 handoffs and confirmed packaging inputs: esbuild bundle around cli.ts, Node 22+ runtime requirement, and current daemon feature surface to preserve
Inspected current daemon package, root package scripts, and workflow/docs entry points; identified missing docs/cockpit-runbook.md path and need to locate the actual runbook file before editing
Chose the packaging implementation: esbuild bundle plus copied host Node 22 runtime and generated install script, so the artifact installs a real cockpit-daemon command without pnpm
Added distribution builder/test files, daemon/root package scripts, CI artifact workflow, and runbook install guidance; next step is doc cleanup plus build/test verification
Verified daemon unit tests and TypeScript build after adding distribution packaging support
Packaging build failed after the esbuild bundle because the tsx script assumed import.meta.dirname; patching the helper scripts to derive their directory from import.meta.url

Installer smoke test failed because the cockpit-daemon launcher resolved paths relative to the symlink in ~/.local/bin instead of the installed package root; patching the launcher to follow symlinks before computing ROOT_DIR
Installer smoke test reached the packaged runtime, but the bundled .cjs file still contained an esbuild-inserted shebang; removing that banner so the wrapper remains the only executable entry point
Verified installed cockpit-daemon status and login from the packaged Linux artifact; switching the daemon smoke assertion to a bounded startup check because the daemon command is long-lived by design
Built the linux-x64 artifact successfully and proved the installed cockpit-daemon command can run status, login, and a bounded daemon startup/shutdown cycle from a fresh PATH without pnpm
Preparing final handoff with changed files, artifact/install contract, verification commands, and packaging limitations for downstream tasks
Wrote the downstream handoff documenting the distribution artifact contract, CI build workflow, verification steps, and remaining platform limitations
